meta.title: Marianne Cottage - Bed & Breakfast in Normandy
meta.description: Charming 1800s farmhouse with 2 bedrooms in the heart of Normandy. Free WiFi, parking, and breakfast included.

hero.tagline: A haven of peace in the heart of Normandy

about.heading: Welcome to Marianne Cottage
about.p1: Nestled in the peaceful Normandy countryside, Marianne Cottage is a beautifully restored 1800s farmhouse set on a quiet lane just a few kilometres from some of France's most significant historical sites. Our intimate bed and breakfast offers the perfect base for exploring the region while providing the comfort and tranquility you deserve.
about.p2: Hosts Mark and Kim have lovingly restored this historic property, welcoming guests to experience authentic Normandy hospitality. You'll find yourself surrounded by rolling green fields and enjoy the sounds of nature—from the gentle breeze to the calls of the wildlife that makes this area their home.
about.p3: The cottage sits close to the D-Day beaches and historic abbeys of Normandy, making it an ideal location for history enthusiasts. Whether you're exploring WW2 heritage sites, wandering through medieval villages, or simply soaking in the rural tranquility, Marianne Cottage provides the perfect retreat.

testimonials.heading: Guest Reviews
testimonials.simon: Warm welcome, lovely room, comfortable bed, modern spacious bathroom, excellent breakfast.
testimonials.ingrid: Lacked nothing for 5 days. Immaculate rooms, extensive freshly prepared breakfast, wonderful evening meal option.
testimonials.guest3: Warm welcome, delightful countryside house, peaceful, close to points of interest, made-to-measure breakfast.

attractions_data.dday_desc: Historic D-Day landing beaches and memorials
attractions_data.cerisy_desc: Beautiful medieval abbey in the countryside

rooms.title: Rooms & Amenities
rooms.description: Our comfortable suite with 2 bedrooms and modern bathroom

suite.heading: Spacious 2-Bedroom Suite
suite.description: Our suite features two bedrooms, a modern walk-in shower bathroom with quality toiletries, and beautiful garden views. Comfortable beds, fresh linens, and thoughtful touches make this the perfect home away from home.

breakfast.heading: Breakfast
breakfast.description: Enjoy a delicious buffet and continental breakfast each morning, featuring fresh croissants and baguettes from our local boulangerie, seasonal fruits, juice, yoghurt, and homemade jams. Special requests (such as eggs) are happily accommodated upon request.

evening_meal.heading: Evening Meal
evening_meal.description: Available by arrangement. Mark prepares a home-cooked dinner from €20 per person. Let us know your preferences when you book!

directions.heading: Getting Here
directions.important: Important: Please arrive via the village of Couvains. Many satellite navigation systems route via the Belleval road, which is a poorly maintained potholed farm track after the first 75 metres. Follow signs through Couvains village instead.

explore.title: Explore Normandy
explore.description: Marianne Cottage is ideally located for exploring the rich history and natural beauty of Normandy. Below you'll find attractions ranging from WW2 heritage sites to charming medieval villages and natural landmarks.
explore.omaha_desc: Historic D-Day landing beaches and memorials
explore.la_cambe_desc: World War II German military cemetery
explore.overlord_desc: Comprehensive D-Day and WW2 history museum
explore.cerisy_desc: Beautiful medieval abbey surrounded by nature
explore.haras_desc: Historic stud farm with beautiful gardens
explore.saintlo_desc: Historic medieval town with remarkable architecture
explore.bayeux_desc: Famous medieval town with stunning cathedral

footer.tagline: A haven of peace in the heart of Normandy

omaha_beach.title: Omaha Beach
omaha_beach.subtitle: The most iconic D-Day landing site
omaha_beach.summary: One of five Allied landing beaches on D-Day, 6 June 1944, Omaha saw the fiercest fighting of the Normandy landings. Today, the wide sandy beach and nearby memorials stand as a powerful tribute to the thousands who gave their lives. A visit here is a profoundly moving experience.

american_cemetery.title: Normandy American Cemetery
american_cemetery.subtitle: 9,387 graves overlooking Omaha Beach
american_cemetery.summary: Perched on a clifftop above Omaha Beach, the Normandy American Cemetery is one of the most visited sites in France. Row upon row of gleaming white crosses and Stars of David mark the graves of American soldiers who fell during the D-Day campaign. The visitor centre tells their individual stories.

pointe_du_hoc.title: Pointe du Hoc
pointe_du_hoc.subtitle: Where Rangers scaled the cliffs under fire
pointe_du_hoc.summary: Preserved almost exactly as it was left in 1944, Pointe du Hoc is the most dramatic WW2 site in Normandy. US Army Rangers scaled these 30-metre cliffs under enemy fire to silence German gun batteries. The bomb craters and shattered bunkers remain eerily intact.

bayeux.title: Bayeux
bayeux.subtitle: Medieval town, tapestry and cathedral
bayeux.summary: Bayeux holds a unique place in history: the first French town liberated after D-Day, and home to the world-famous 11th-century Bayeux Tapestry depicting the Norman Conquest of England. The town's magnificent Gothic cathedral and charming medieval streets make it essential visiting.

arromanches.title: Arromanches
arromanches.subtitle: Where the artificial harbour changed history
arromanches.summary: In June 1944, the Allies constructed a remarkable prefabricated harbour at Arromanches — a feat of engineering that allowed millions of tonnes of supplies to be landed. Remnants of the Mulberry harbour are still visible from the beach. The Arromanches 360° cinema brings the story to life.

memorial_caen.title: Mémorial de Caen
memorial_caen.subtitle: The leading WW2 museum in France
memorial_caen.summary: Opened in 1988, the Mémorial de Caen is widely regarded as France's finest WW2 museum. Built on the site of a German command post, it traces the road to war, the Occupation, and the Battle of Normandy with stunning multimedia exhibitions. Allow a full day for a thorough visit.

utah_beach.title: Utah Beach
utah_beach.subtitle: The most westerly D-Day landing beach
utah_beach.summary: Utah was the westernmost of the five D-Day beaches and saw the most successful Allied landing of 6 June 1944. The excellent Utah Beach Museum traces the day in detail with original vehicles, weapons, and personal accounts. The beach itself is quiet and atmospheric.

la_cambe.title: La Cambe German Cemetery
la_cambe.subtitle: 21,000 German soldiers rest here
la_cambe.summary: In stark contrast to the white crosses of the American Cemetery, La Cambe is a place of sombre beauty. Dark basalt crosses mark the graves of over 21,000 German soldiers. The cemetery is maintained by the German War Graves Commission and invites reflection on the human cost of war on all sides.

overlord_museum.title: Overlord Museum
overlord_museum.subtitle: The finest collection of D-Day vehicles
overlord_museum.summary: Just a short distance from Omaha Beach, the Overlord Museum houses one of the most impressive collections of original WW2 military hardware in Europe. Tanks, half-tracks, aircraft, and personal equipment bring the scale of Operation Overlord into vivid focus.

cerisy_abbey.title: Cerisy Abbey
cerisy_abbey.subtitle: A Benedictine gem on your doorstep
cerisy_abbey.summary: Just minutes from Marianne Cottage, Cerisy Abbey is one of Normandy's best-kept secrets. Founded in 1032 by Duke Robert I of Normandy, father of William the Conqueror, its Romanesque church is remarkably well-preserved. The peaceful grounds and surrounding forest make it a wonderful half-day visit.

saint_lo.title: Saint-Lô
saint_lo.subtitle: The 'capital of ruins' reborn
saint_lo.summary: Saint-Lô was almost entirely destroyed in July 1944 and was famously described as 'the capital of ruins.' Rebuilt with striking post-war architecture, it is now the lively capital of the Manche department. Visit the stud farm, the restored town walls, and the moving ruins of Notre-Dame church.

haras_national.title: Haras National de Saint-Lô
haras_national.subtitle: Normandy's finest stud farm
haras_national.summary: The National Stud of Saint-Lô is one of the most prestigious in France, home to around 80 stallions of various breeds. Guided tours reveal the beautiful 18th-century stable buildings, and summer equestrian shows are a highlight of the local calendar. A unique slice of Norman culture.

chateau_balleroy.title: Château de Balleroy
chateau_balleroy.subtitle: A 17th-century château and balloon museum
chateau_balleroy.summary: Built between 1626 and 1636 for Jean de Choisy, Château de Balleroy is considered one of the finest early French classical châteaux. The grounds include an excellent hot-air balloon museum — the late Malcolm Forbes held his famous balloon meets here. The formal gardens are open throughout the season.

coutances.title: Coutances
coutances.subtitle: Gothic cathedral soaring over the bocage
coutances.summary: Coutances is dominated by its magnificent Gothic cathedral, widely considered one of the finest in Normandy and a masterpiece of 13th-century architecture. The town's botanical gardens are among the most beautiful in the region. The weekly market fills the streets with local produce.

carentan.title: Carentan
carentan.subtitle: Gateway to Utah Beach and the marshes
carentan.summary: Carentan sits at the heart of the Normandy marshlands and was the scene of fierce fighting in June 1944 as US paratroopers of the 101st Airborne linked the Utah and Omaha beachheads. Today it is a pleasant market town and marina, a great base for exploring the Marais du Cotentin nature park.

juno_beach.title: Juno Beach Centre
juno_beach.subtitle: Canada's tribute to its D-Day veterans
juno_beach.summary: The Juno Beach Centre at Courseulles-sur-Mer is Canada's national memorial in Normandy, dedicated to the 45,000 Canadians who died in the Second World War. Interactive exhibitions tell the story of the Canadian home front and the Juno Beach landings with exceptional personal testimony.

lessay_abbey.title: Lessay Abbey
lessay_abbey.subtitle: The purest Romanesque church in Normandy
lessay_abbey.summary: Lessay Abbey is widely regarded as the finest example of Romanesque architecture in Normandy. Founded in 1056, it was almost entirely destroyed in 1944 and meticulously rebuilt using original materials. The result is a building of extraordinary purity and calm. The September Sainte-Croix fair is one of Normandy's oldest traditions.

vire.title: Vire
vire.subtitle: Gorges, andouille and bocage country
vire.summary: Perched above the spectacular Vire gorges, this compact town is the heart of the Norman bocage. Vire is famous throughout France for its andouille sausage, made by artisan charcutiers following centuries-old recipes. The surrounding countryside offers superb walking and cycling through unspoilt hedgerow landscape.

torigni_sur_vire.title: Torigni-sur-Vire
torigni_sur_vire.subtitle: A château town in the heart of Normandy
torigni_sur_vire.summary: The small town of Torigni-sur-Vire is dominated by its imposing 16th-century château, now home to local government and open to visitors in summer. The town makes a pleasant stop with a weekly market and easy access to walking routes along the Vire valley.

mont_saint_michel.title: Mont Saint-Michel
mont_saint_michel.subtitle: One of France's most iconic landmarks
mont_saint_michel.summary: Rising dramatically from the sea at the boundary of Normandy and Brittany, Mont Saint-Michel is one of the most visited sites in France and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The medieval abbey perched on its rocky island is breathtaking at any tide. Allow a full day and, if possible, arrive at dusk.
